_about: Canonical, controlled facet vocabulary for the difficulty auto-tuning system. SOURCE OF TRUTH: the add-to-backlog authoring step picks facet values ONLY from this file; the calibration/policy validate against it; a human curates it (deliberate additions only, never ad-hoc per task). Facets are the join key that lets the policy generalise escalation history from past tasks to new ones. needs-human (gated) tasks are CARVED OUT entirely — they never run through the loop, so they get no facets and never enter calibration. ⚠️ The 'difficultyHint' on each value is ADVISORY ONLY — human/LLM-readable taxonomy intuition; the policy NEVER reads it. Model selection is driven entirely by the outcomes ledger (.harness/outcomes.jsonl), the tier ladder (.tiers.ladder), and the floor/minN knobs (.policy). Do NOT wire difficultyHint into any code.

layer.desc: WHERE the change lives — inferable largely from the task's scope file paths.

work-type.desc: WHAT KIND of change it is — the main thing the authoring LLM reasons about.

risk.desc: Danger modifiers; each pushes difficulty UP. Used by the policy only once a cell is dense.

touches-schema.desc: scope includes src/db/schema.sql or src/db/index.ts (the T098 trap)

touches-executor.desc: scope includes core/executor, workflow-executor, dag, or scheduler

full-stack.desc: spans db + api + dashboard in one task

policy._about: Difficulty auto-tuning policy knobs. The policy picks, per task, the CHEAPEST tier on the ladder below whose historical first-attempt success rate for the task's (layer × work-type) cell is >= floor, requiring at least minN samples; otherwise it falls back to the authored difficulty (cold start). Bias is toward cheap — the escalation ladder is the safety net.
policy._audit: Verification-aware calibration (the blocking audit gate — .harness/designs/audit-verification.md). Per facet-cell, the loop audits 100% until auditStartN audit-confirmed successes, tapering to auditFloor by auditFloorN. The auditor runs at max(auditorModel/auditorEffort, builder tier). Separate knobs from floor/minN (model selection).

tiers._about: The GLOBAL difficulty ladder, cheapest → most expensive. The policy chooses a START tier from here per task; escalation walks UP it. The authored model/effort is the cold-start start tier. Project-specific (override per project).
